prompt: Two things landed on the gate-badge crate at once and now CI is red. First, we made badge numbers typed last week — `BadgeId<Visitor>` vs `BadgeId<Contractor>` so a contractor badge physically can't be passed to the visitor sign-out — and cargo now refuses to build with `error[E0392]: type parameter T is never used`. The typed API is the whole point and the tests use it, so making the struct non-generic again is not an acceptable answer; make the parameter legitimate the way the compiler suggests. Second, the new CI gate (`bash run_verify.sh`, which sets `RUSTFLAGS="-W unused-lifetimes -D warnings"`) also trips over lifetime parameters someone declared and never used in the render module, plus a handful of stale `use` lines scattered across the modules from before the registry rewrite. None of this should change behavior — the tests pin the current badge numbering, sign-out semantics, and printed label layout exactly. Clean it all up so `bash run_verify.sh` passes; tests/gate.rs and run_verify.sh are off limits.
notes: 5W2 item 3: unused-lifetime + elision + unused type param (PhantomData decision) + unused imports across a module tree. rustc 1.96.0. Planted: E0392 on id::BadgeId<T> (raw u32 only) — hard error at baseline; prompt pins the generic API (tests type-annotate BadgeId<Visitor>/BadgeId<Contractor>), so the fix is a PhantomData field (reference uses `_kind: PhantomData<T>`; PhantomData<fn() -> T> equally valid — tests only need Copy/Clone/PartialEq to keep deriving, which marker types satisfy); unused_lifetimes x2 — render::badge_line<'a> and render::day_header<'h> declare lifetimes never used (lint is allow-by-default, probed; run_verify.sh raises it with -W unused-lifetimes then -D warnings makes it fatal; rustc's help says 'elide the unused lifetime'); unused_imports x3 across the module tree — id.rs `use std::fmt`, registry.rs `use std::collections::HashSet`, render.rs `use std::collections::BTreeMap`. Note the id.rs fix REPLACES the dead fmt import with the PhantomData import — teacher must not just delete lines. Verified post-fix that -W unused-lifetimes is honored through RUSTFLAGS (gate fails when a lifetime is re-added). Baseline fails at E0392 regardless of flags. Domain: depot front-gate visitor badges — distinct from py-visitpass (python, visit passes business logic) by language, layer (typed-id generics), and defect class (warnings, not logic).
